name: subtract_aligned_preserves
code:
proof fn phi_1_subtract_aligned_preserves(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, c),
        aligned(b, c),
        a <= b,
        c > 0,
    ensures
        aligned((b - a) as nat, c),
{
    subtract_mod_eq_zero(a, b, c);
}
reason: Difference of two aligned values is aligned — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: subtract_self_aligned
reason: Subtracting a value from itself yields 0, which is trivially aligned — tests the `a == b` boundary

name: subtract_zero_aligned
reason: Subtracting 0 preserves alignment — tests the `a == 0` boundary (tautological)

name: subtract_consecutive_multiples
reason: Difference of any two multiples of `c` is aligned to `c` — tests closure of multiples under subtraction

name: vstd_lemma_sub_mod_noop_trusted
reason: The proof depends on vstd's `lemma_sub_mod_noop` — if that library lemma were unsound the subtraction-alignment property would be wrong
